source_depth_extraction_path: PyMuPDF text layer, direct clause-level read

financial_obligations.text_summary: On or before the 6th calendar day of each month, Franchisee shall pay Royalty Fee equal to 7% of Gross Revenue for the previous month
Franchisor has right (but not obligation) to collect payment on behalf of designated third-party supplier and impose a reasonable markup or administrative charge
Franchisor has right to collect a technology fee at any point of the Initial Term, may use for any technology-related purpose, and may raise fee at any time during Initial Term

Franchisor may require Franchisee, owners, CEO, and School Administrator to attend conferences and charge per-person attendance fee. Franchisee pays all travel, food, transportation, lodging. Non-attendance still charged.

Franchisor reserves right to charge fees for goods/services supplied by Franchisor or affiliates; sole discretion to change fee amounts and add/delete/modify goods and services at any time. 90 days prior to opening, Franchisee pays then-current décor package fee

1.5% per month (or highest state-allowed rate, whichever is lower) on all Royalty Fees, Brand Fund Contributions, and amounts due for purchases from Franchisor and affiliates. Franchisee also pays all franchisor costs to collect unpaid fees

Up to $1,000 per week plus franchisor costs and expenses for ongoing non-compliance until franchisee ceases; framed as 'reasonable estimate of franchisor's internal cost' and 'additional to all franchisor's other rights and remedies'

Notwithstanding any designation by Franchisee, franchisor and affiliates have the right to apply any franchisee payment to any past-due indebtedness in any proportion or priority (Royalty, Brand Fund, purchases, or any other amount owed to franchisor or affiliates)

eft_autodebit_mechanics.text_verbatim_short: Franchisee shall pay the Royalty Fee, Brand Fund Contribution, and any other amounts owed to Franchisor or its Affiliates by pre-authorized bank draft or in such other manner as Franchisor or its Affiliates may require. Franchisor may adjust the due date of any fee or amount.

lien_security_interest.explicit_security_interest_in_franchisee: Not directly surfaced in Exhibit A as extracted (no 'security interest' or UCC-1 clause encountered in Exhibit A body text). Exhibit F Unlimited Guaranty is an unlimited personal guaranty of owners' obligations rather than a collateral pledge.
lien_security_interest.franchisor_right_to_take_lease_assignmen: Yes — Item 8 body and FA lease rider require landlord to grant franchisor the right at franchisor's option and without compensation to take assignment of the lease if franchisee materially defaults under the lease or if the franchise terminates or is not renewed
lien_security_interest.set_off_right: Franchisor and affiliates shall set off sums franchisor or its affiliates owes franchisee or its affiliates against any past-due indebtedness as franchisor deems appropriate (section 3.11 area)

data_ownership_and_access_rights.text_summary: Franchisee maintains a data set of customer names, purchase/enrollment history, home addresses, email, phone, plus permissions/consents. Franchisee shall use Technology that franchisor designates to create, store, maintain, and share the Customer List. The Customer List shall be the property of Franchisor. Franchisor shall have the right to use the Customer List for any purposes.

All data 'collectively referred to herein as Franchised Business Data is deemed to be owned by Franchisor'. Franchisee agrees to furnish Franchised Business Data to franchisor at any time franchisor requests. Franchisee must use the Technology that franchisor designates to create, store, maintain, and share the data. Franchisor grants a limited license to franchisee to use Franchised Business Data while the Agreement is in effect.

Franchisee shall comply with, or adopt policies consistent with, franchisor's then-current Data Protection and Security Policies as described in the Manual. Policies may govern how Franchised Business Data and Personal Information are collected, used, stored, processed, shared, or destroyed. 'Personal Information' defined as any information that can identify, locate, or contact an individual or household and any information defined as protected personal information under any Privacy Law.

Franchisor may introduce new technology requirements or modify specifications; no limits on franchisor's rights. Franchisor may sublicense technology to franchisee and charge all amounts paid to supplier based on franchisee's use plus reasonable markup. Franchisor is not obligated to provide maintenance, repair, upgrades, or updates and disclaims all liability for malfunctions, crashes, or data loss.

Franchisor has the right at all times to independently access franchisee technology to retrieve, analyze, and use information, including financial information, without first notifying franchisee. No contractual limits on this right, regardless of whether franchisee was required to purchase the technology.

At franchisor's request, franchisee shall authorize and direct any third parties, including accounting and legal professionals, to release to franchisor all accounting and financial records arising from or relating to the operation of the Franchised Business

system_change_exposure.text_summary: Franchisor has the right, in its sole discretion, to change or modify the System at any time including adoption of new or modified Marks, copyrighted materials, curricula, teaching materials, computer hardware, software, equipment, inventory, supplies, or sales and marketing techniques. Franchisee shall accept and use such changes as if they were part of the Agreement as of the Effective Date. Franchisee must make such expenditures as such changes require. Franchisor acknowledges that required changes may require significant capital expenditures 'in an amount that is unknown on the Effective Date and that cannot be fully amortized over the period of time then remaining in the Initial Term.'

Franchisor has the right to vary standards or specifications for any franchisee based upon that franchisee's qualifications, site peculiarities, demographics, business potential, existing business practices, or any other condition franchisor deems important. (No obligation to treat franchisees uniformly on standards.)

remodel_rebuild_refurbish_obligations.text_summary: Franchisor 'reserves the right to require Franchisee to generally refurbish the Franchised Business at Franchisee's expense' to conform to building design, trade dress, color schemes, System, and presentation of the Marks consistent with then-current image for Ivybrook Academy franchises. Includes 'structural changes, installation of new materials and equipment, remodeling, redecoration, changing color schemes, and modifications and/or repairs to existing improvements.' May include replacing worn out, obsolete, or dated equipment, fixtures, furnishings.

As a condition of renewal, 'Franchisee has, at its expense, made such capital expenditures as were necessary to maintain uniformity with any Franchisor-required System modifications such that the Franchised Business reflects Franchisor's then-current standards and specifications'

Franchisee constructs or remodels premises in accordance with franchisor's Construction Standards; franchisee solely responsible for construction; franchisee must obtain franchisor's acceptance of proposed plans; must commence construction promptly after lease execution; must maintain continuous construction until completion

appraisal_fmv_mechanics.clause: 17.2 Post-termination franchisor option to purchase assets
appraisal_fmv_mechanics.text_summary: During 60-day period after termination, non-renewal, or expiration, franchisor may purchase any assets of the Franchised Business at fair market value

appraisal_fmv_mechanics.clause: Lease rider — rent determination by independent appraiser upon franchisor assumption
appraisal_fmv_mechanics.text_summary: 'If the parties are not able to agree on an independent appraiser within 45 days of the termination, non-renewal, or expiration of this Agreement, each party will select an independent [appraiser]'; rent will be determined by qualified independent appraiser; franchisee and franchisor each present proposed rent; independent appraiser selects the most commercially reasonable rent from the two proposals; appraiser's determination binding on parties

tax_permit_indebtedness_burdens.clause: Franchisee records include sales tax records and state/federal tax returns
tax_permit_indebtedness_burdens.text_summary: Franchisee shall maintain purchase orders, invoices, payroll records, sales tax records, state and federal tax returns, bank statements, cancelled checks, deposit receipts, cash receipts and disbursements. Franchisee shall submit copies of all state sales tax returns to franchisor.
Franchisee is responsible for conforming the site to local ordinances and building codes and obtaining any required permits

Lease rider provides for subordination to any mortgage, deed of trust, deed to secure debt, or similar encumbrance on the premises that the holder agrees not to disturb tenant's rights (standard SNDA language)

estoppel_certificate_obligation.search_result: No occurrence of 'estoppel' found in Exhibit A text extraction.
estoppel_certificate_obligation.note: Estoppel certificate clause not directly surfaced in the franchise agreement body. Typical lease riders sometimes include an estoppel duty but it is not captured in the extracted text layer.

entity_guarantor_structure_requirements.text_summary: Franchisee's articles of incorporation, articles of organization, or written partnership agreement shall at all times provide that Franchisee's activities are limited exclusively to the development and operation of the IVYBROOK ACADEMY Franchised Business

If Franchisee is a corporation, Franchisee shall maintain stop-transfer instructions against the transfer on its records of any voting securities, and each stock certificate (must bear a restrictive legend)

If franchisee is a corporation, limited liability company, or partnership, franchisee has provided franchisor a current list of all owners and agrees to advise franchisor of any and all changes in ownership

Franchisee shall appoint a CEO (or similar title) as primary executive with decision-making authority. If franchisee is an individual, then franchisee shall be CEO. CEO must have at least 10% ownership interest. CEO must be present at the Franchised Business no less than 20 hours per week during the first year of operations, excluding vacation/sick leave.

If franchisee is an entity, franchisor may require any Related Party (including owners, members, partners, directors, officers, principals) to sign this Agreement and/or separate agreements for the purpose of binding the Related Party
Owners execute an Unlimited Guaranty and Assumption of Obligations — joint and several personal guaranty of all franchisee obligations under the Franchise Agreement
CEO, School Administrator, owners, and certain designated personnel sign Nondisclosure/Non-Solicitation Agreement and Nondisclosure/Non-Competition Agreement

insurance_minimums_surfaced.coverage: Comprehensive general liability (bodily injury, personal injury, death, property damage)
insurance_minimums_surfaced.minimum: $2,000,000 per occurrence or statutory minimum, whichever higher
Required (amount not specified as dollar minimum)
Procure 60 days prior to Franchised Business opening date

All policies (except workers comp) must name franchisor, affiliates, officers, directors, shareholders, members, managers, employees, successors, and assigns as additional insured or loss payee; waiver of all subrogation rights; primary and non-contributory basis; insurer must defend both franchisee and franchisor

choice_of_law.text_summary: North Carolina law, without reference to conflict of laws principles

mediation_required_before_arbitration.text_summary: Before franchisee or its owners/affiliates and franchisor or its affiliates may bring an Action, parties must first meet to mediate. Mediation is non-binding. Mediator must have franchise law experience; AAA mediation rules.

injunctive_carveout.text_summary: Franchisor at its option may immediately (without first seeking mediation or arbitration) seek preliminary injunctive relief; nothing bars franchisor's right to obtain injunctive relief, restraining orders, preliminary and permanent injunctions against threatened conduct

jury_trial_waiver.text_summary: Parties irrevocably waive trial by jury in any Action

relationship_and_standard_of_care.text_summary: Agreement is purely a contractual relationship; does not appoint or make franchisee an agent, legal representative, joint venturer, partner, employee, servant, or independent contractor of franchisor. Franchisee may not represent itself as franchisor's agent

Agreement does not establish a fiduciary relationship. Unless otherwise specifically provided, whenever Agreement requires franchisor's written consent or permits franchisor to take or refrain from action, franchisor is free to act in its own self-interest without any obligation to act reasonably, to consider impact on franchisee, or to act subject to any other standard of care limiting franchisor's right, except as may be provided by statute or regulation. No covenant of good faith and fair dealing shall be implied into this Agreement except to the extent required by law.

non_compliance_auto_charge_stack.summary: Franchisee is exposed to a cumulative stack of franchisor-initiated charges that can run concurrently: (1) 1.5% per month late interest (3.9); (2) $1,000/week noncompliance fee (3.10); (3) Up to $1,000 per incident late reporting fine (12.9); (4) Cure-cost markup of franchisor out-of-pocket + internal allocation + 10% (Item 6); (5) Audit cost recovery of $1,500–$5,000 plus franchisor's right to apply any franchisee payment to any past-due indebtedness in any priority (3.11); plus (6) all collection costs and attorneys' fees
